FDV/Market Cap above use DexScreener's own supply estimate, which can differ from this report's own on-chain-verified total supply (via a live totalSupply() call) and from its own circulating-supply calculation further down in the Supply Concentration section (total minus confirmed burned and LP-locked tokens). These are three independently-correct numbers using three different definitions of "supply" — not a data error if they don't match exactly.

Filtered to holders with ≥$10k USD value at current price, no ENTITY_LABELS match, and not a known CEX hot wallet. Click any row to open the wallet drawer.
Alerts Since Last Run
Changes detected by comparing this report against the previous snapshot (siren_snapshot.json)
0 alert(s)
Type Wallet Detail
No changes detected vs previous run.
Alert types: New Large Holder (unlabeled wallet entered top 250 with ≥$15k), Balance Change (±25%+ vs last run), Exchange Deposit (wallet sent to known CEX hot wallet since last run). Runs automatically each time the analyzer generates a report.
Supply Concentration & Reconciliation
Computed from the full verified holder list and real on-chain total supply
No complete holder balance list or on-chain total supply was available this run — concentration metrics need both, so they're skipped rather than estimated from a partial candidate pool.
Gini Coefficient
0.997
Top 10 Wallets
76.8%
Top 50 Wallets
85.5%
Top 250 Wallets
93.7%
Holders Tracked
64,828
Reconciliation
Reconciled
Gini: 0 = perfectly even distribution, 1 = one wallet holds everything. Top N % is measured against circulating supply (704,945,337 tokens = total supply 1,000,000,000 − burned − LP-locked). Reconciliation compares the sum of every tracked holder's balance against that same circulating-supply figure — a gap beyond ±1% (+0.01% here) usually means a stale holder export or an unlabeled pool/locker contract, not a real loss of tokens.

Derivatives — SIRENUSDT Perpetual
Real data from Binance Futures public API, no estimates
SIRENUSDT not currently live on Binance Futures (or the API was unreachable when this report was generated) — derivatives charts skipped rather than showing fabricated numbers. Liquidations / liquidation heatmap are not shown for any state: Binance only publishes liquidation data for thin-liquidity symbols, and a "heatmap" from any other free source would just be a leverage-tier model dressed up as measured data.

Excludes the burn address and the DEX liquidity pool. Burned: 275,597,073 tokens · LP-locked: 19,457,590 tokens.
Methodology & Limitations ▾

Data sources. Transfers: Moralis (chunked by date, resumable across daily quota limits). Ground-truth holder balances: a BscScan "Export Token Holders" CSV dropped into the project folder, with public BSC RPC (bsc-dataseed.binance.org, rpc.ankr.com/bsc, bsc-rpc.publicnode.com) as a live fallback for anything the CSV doesn't cover. Price history: GeckoTerminal (daily OHLCV, keyed by the top liquidity pool). Market snapshot: DexScreener. Derivatives: Binance Futures' public API for SIRENUSDT. Total supply: a live on-chain totalSupply() call. All free, all keyless except Moralis.

Verified vs. inferred. Balances, price history, market snapshot, derivatives, never-sold wallets, exchange-inflow labels, burned/LP-locked token counts, total supply, and the concentration/cohort metrics are all real data pulled directly from the sources above — nothing here is estimated. The one exception is the Wallet Behavior (Bot vs. Trader) classification: that's an inference from transaction frequency and timing regularity, shown with a confidence level, and a very active human can trigger the same signal. It is the only heuristic label in this report, and it's marked as such everywhere it appears.

Known limitations. The transfer dataset can be partial if Moralis's daily quota was hit mid-fetch — check the Dataset Status panel; a partial run resumes automatically next time. Price-history coverage may not reach all the way back to launch, since GeckoTerminal's free OHLCV endpoint has its own window; USD figures for trades outside that window are left blank (marked •) rather than priced at $0. Concentration, Gini, and the supply-reconciliation check require both a complete holder balance list and a successful on-chain total-supply call — if either is missing, that section is skipped rather than computed from a partial candidate pool. A shared-funder "wallet cluster" bubble map was deliberately left out of this report in favor of pointing to a dedicated tool (Bubblemaps) for that kind of entity-clustering analysis, rather than approximating it.
